body:
Follow-up from the #292 mixed-era placement audit (item 1 of 5 ŌĆö **priority**). Sibling of #287 (same failure class, in the drain path).

## Problem

Rebalance evacuation planning does not apply the identity-placement fallback the read path, GC, scrub, and reconstruction all use. A pre-M3 / mixed-era `ChunkRef` decodes with `placement: vec![]` (`#[serde(default)]`, `crates/core/src/metadata.rs:93`), and the read path resolves its fragment `i` to D-server `i`. Rebalance does not ŌĆö so a fragment that is live and readable can be left on a draining D-server and **lost on decommission**.

## Root cause

`plan_evacuations` (`crates/custodian/src/rebalance.rs:150-160`) iterates the raw vector:

```rust
let evac: Vec<usize> = chunk
    .placement
    .iter()                              // empty for a pre-M3 chunk
    .enumerate()
    .filter(|(_, server)| draining.contains(server))
    .map(|(index, _)| index)
    .collect();
if evac.is_empty() {
    continue;                            // ŌåÆ pre-M3 chunk skipped entirely
}
```

For an empty `placement` the iterator yields nothing, `evac` is empty, and the chunk is skipped ŌĆö no `EvacPlan` is produced. The `survivor_domains` computation just below (`rebalance.rs:163-169`) reads the raw vector too. `placed_dserver`'s doc comment (`metadata.rs:110-118`) lists read/GC/scrub/reconstruction as its callers and omits rebalance ŌĆö the omission is the tell.

## Impact

Latent data loss during mixed-era operation (pre-M3 chunks not yet rewritten by any reconstruction/rebalance) when a D-server is drained/decommissioned:

- `EcScheme::None` (M0 `replication(1)`): the chunk's only fragment is never moved ŌåÆ the object becomes unreadable.
- `ReedSolomon`: silently erodes redundancy; repeated drains can cross `m`.

The operator sees the drain "complete" while pre-M3 fragments were left behind. Trigger window is migration time ŌĆö exactly when drains are most likely.

## Fix

In `plan_evacuations`, expand each chunk over `0..chunk.fragment_count()` and resolve each fragment via `chunk.placed_dserver(index)` (or the shared `ChunkRef::fragments()` helper if that lands first ŌĆö see the sibling helper issue), so the set of fragments considered for evacuation equals the read path's resolved placement closure. Apply the same expansion to the `survivor_domains` computation so spread is preserved correctly for mixed-era chunks.


**Not just the filter ŌĆö materialize the placement vector too** (verified in the #292 follow-up audit). `EvacPlan.placement` is stored raw and consumed downstream in `evacuate_chunk`: `rebalance.rs:221` (`new_placement = plan.placement.clone()`), `:224` (`plan.placement[index]`), `:245` (`new_placement[index] = target`), `:253` (the version-conditional write `next_chunk_map[...].placement = new_placement`). With a pre-M3 **empty** `placement`, cloning + indexing it **panics** (index out of bounds); a **short** vector writes back a malformed short placement. So expanding only the evac filter + `survivor_domains` is a *half-fix* ŌĆö it selects the right fragment to move but then panics or corrupts the record when it commits the move. The fix must **materialize a full-length, identity-resolved placement vector** (length `fragment_count()`, via `0..n` ŌåÆ `placed_dserver(i)`) into `EvacPlan.placement` before evacuation, so the commit writes a complete, correct M3 placement record. The regression test must therefore assert the **committed placement is full-length and repointed off the draining server** (length == `fragment_count()`), not merely that an `EvacPlan` is produced.

## Test

Regression mirroring the #287 GC cases (`crates/custodian/tests/gc.rs`): commit an inode whose `ChunkRef` has `placement: vec![]`, mark the identity-resolved D-server draining, run the rebalance loop, and assert an `EvacPlan` is produced that moves the fragment to a healthy non-draining distinct-domain server. Fails before the fix (no plan), passes after. Add `EcScheme::None` and a `ReedSolomon` case (fragment at index > 0).

milestone.title: M3 ŌĆö Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: Rebalance evacuation ignores identity-placement fallback ŌĆö pre-M3 fragments on a draining D-server are never evacuated
